title: Add Feature Request / Bug Report Form with Admin-Visible Submissions

requirements.text: Add a backend data type `FeedbackSubmission` with fields: id (Nat), submitterPrincipal (Text), submitterUserId (Text), submissionType (variant { FeatureRequest; BugReport }), title (Text), description (Text), timestamp (Int). Store submissions in a stable array or HashMap. Expose the following functions: `submitFeedback(submissionType, title, description)` callable by any authenticated user (captures caller principal and derived user ID automatically); `getFeedbackSubmissions()` callable only by admins and owner, returns all submissions; `deleteFeedbackSubmission(id)` callable only by admins and owner.

quotes: stores the data in a list the admins and owner can see
That shows their user id when sent

acceptanceCriteria: submitFeedback stores the caller's principal and derived user ID (8-char base-36 hash) alongside submission data
getFeedbackSubmissions returns all submissions only when caller is admin or owner, otherwise returns an error/empty
deleteFeedbackSubmission removes a submission by ID only when caller is admin or owner
Submissions are persisted in stable storage across upgrades

requirements.text: Add a 'Feedback' button or link in the main navigation/header (Layout.tsx area, but implement via App.tsx or a new modal/page) that is visible to all authenticated users. This opens a modal form with: a toggle/tab to select 'Request a Feature' or 'Report a Bug'; a Title field; a Description textarea; and a Submit button. On submission, call the backend `submitFeedback` function. Show a success toast on completion and close the modal.

quotes: Add a simple "Request a Feature" or "Report a Bug" form

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Feedback' button is visible in the header/nav for all authenticated users
The modal contains a type selector (Feature Request / Bug Report), Title input, and Description textarea
Submitting calls the backend and shows a success toast
The modal closes after successful submission
Form validates that title and description are non-empty before submitting

requirements.text: Add a `useFeedbackSubmit` mutation hook that calls `actor.submitFeedback` and invalidates relevant queries on success. Add a `useFeedbackSubmissions` query hook that calls `actor.getFeedbackSubmissions` (used only in the admin view). Add a `useDeleteFeedback` mutation hook that calls `actor.deleteFeedbackSubmission`.
acceptanceCriteria: useDeleteFeedback mutation hook wraps actor.deleteFeedbackSubmission and invalidates the submissions query on success

requirements.text: Add a 'Feedback Submissions' section to the AdminPanel component. This section displays all submitted feedback in a table/card list showing: submission type badge (Feature Request / Bug Report), title, description, submitter's short user ID, submitter's full principal, and timestamp. Admins and owner can delete individual submissions. Show loading and empty states appropriately.

quotes: stores the data in a list the admins and owner can see
That shows their user id when sent

acceptanceCriteria: The AdminPanel shows a Feedback Submissions section
Each submission displays type badge, title, description, short user ID, full principal, and formatted timestamp
Admins can delete submissions with a confirmation step or delete button
Empty state message is shown when no submissions exist
Section is only visible to admins and owner (gated by existing isOwner/isAdmin checks)

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Do not modify Layout.tsx directly for the Feedback button — wire it through App.tsx state or a wrapper component instead
Access control for getFeedbackSubmissions and deleteFeedbackSubmission must reuse existing admin/owner check patterns already present in backend/main.mo
nonGoals: Sending email notifications — store submissions in backend only
Public visibility of feedback submissions (non-admin users cannot view others' submissions)
